summary: path-internal: typosquat of Node.js 'path' with import-time remote eval
details: On require(), path-internal executes an IIFE that base64-decodes a hardcoded URL (https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/TPQHE, an anonymous JSON paste host), fetches the response, and passes the JSON `content` field directly to eval(). The URL is held in a variable misleadingly named `randomStringRe` to resemble a regex; a second base64 string `tokenStringRe` decodes to https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/HI4TR (a staged/rotatable second endpoint). No integrity check, no pinning — whoever controls the paste can ship arbitrary JavaScript to every consumer at import time. The package's README states it is 'an exact copy of the NodeJS path module' and the name typosquats Node.js's built-in `path`, so any developer who installs `path-internal` by mistake or via dependency-confusion executes operator-controlled remote code in their build/runtime environment.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
